title: Understanding Momentum Change
text: To find the force exerted on the wall, consider the change in momentum of the water as it hits the wall.

title: Momentum Formula
text: The momentum of an object is given by the formula: momentum $$=$$ mass * velocity.

title: Change in Momentum
text: Since the water's horizontal momentum is reduced to zero, the change in momentum is equal to the initial momentum of the water.

title: Force and Momentum Change
text: The force exerted on the wall can be calculated using the formula: force $$=$$ change in momentum / time.

title: Calculate Initial Momentum
text: Calculate the initial momentum of the water using the formula: momentum $$=$$ mass * velocity. Use the given mass flow rate and velocity.

title: Calculate Force
text: Using the change in momentum and the time interval, calculate the force exerted on the wall.
